title: Affirm Holdings Q2 2026 Earnings: Profitability Milestone
text: Affirm Holdings Q2 2026 Earnings: Profitability Milestone Tests Growth Narrative. Key Financial Metrics: Affirm reported significant profitability improvements in Q2 2026. The company achieved quarterly revenue of US$1.1b and basic EPS of US$0.39, with trailing twelve-month figures showing net income of US$282.3 million on revenue of US$3.7b. This represents substantial year-over-year growth, as revenue climbed from US$866.4 million in Q2 2025 to the current US$1.1 billion figure, while earnings per share increased from US$0.25 to US$0.39. Growth Prospects vs. Forecasts: Revenue expansion is tracking ahead of some projections. Analysts forecast 19.1% annual revenue growth, yet bullish investors argue that new products like the Affirm Card and AI-driven underwriting could accelerate this trajectory. However, observers note that any moderation from the recent trajectory would start to chip away at those targets. Earnings forecasts project approximately 31.4% annual growth through 2029, reaching roughly US$1.2 billion in net income. Valuation Concerns: A significant tension exists between current market prices and fundamental measures. The stock trades at a trailing P/E of 79.5x against a DCF fair value estimate of US$40.80 per share, with the current share price at US$67.36—substantially above fair value estimates. This elevated multiple far exceeds industry averages of 18.5x, raising questions about downside risk if growth assumptions moderate.
